summary: High‑energy 1985 show with fiery post‑drums run, standout Eyes of the World
blurb: The set features a scorching Cryptical > The Other One > Cryptical sandwich and an electrifying post‑drums sequence that rolls through Nobody's Fault But Mine into Truckin' and Smokestack Lightning. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while Brent adds spicy keyboard flair. Overall a solid performance with several high points worth revisiting.
review: On September 3, 1985 at the Starlight Theater, the Dead delivered an electric night that still resonates with fans. The first set rolled through classic numbers like Feel Like A Stranger and Dire Wolf before launching into a daring second‑set suite: Cryptical Envelopment segued into The Other One and back again, then burst into Eyes Of The World. Though some listeners felt the eye jam ran a touch fast, the sequence remains a highlight alongside Brent’s quirky vocal interlude “Don’t Need Love”. After the drums and space, the band surged with Nobody's Fault But Mine, Truckin', Smokestack Lightning, and a heartfelt Comes A Time before closing with Turn On Your Lovelight and an encore of It’s All Over Now Baby Blue. Jerry’s lead work is consistently praised—hot, on‑fire guitar throughout—while occasional vocal slips in Cryptical are noted. Brent’s keyboard Rooster solo adds spice, and the dual drummers drive the high‑energy moments, especially the post‑drums run that stitches together several bluesy peaks. Bob Weir’s rhythm work earns enthusiastic nods, rounding out a show that balances tight musicianship with the unpredictable joy of ’80 Dead live.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Majority of recordings (6/10) express positive sentiment, highlighting strong guitar work, standout songs, and high energy, while the remaining mixed reviews note only isolated issues (Cryptical vocals, occasional rushed jams). The overall consensus is clearly favorable, warranting a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, clear mix; no drop‑outs and superb balance that lets the guitar interplay and keyboard solos shine.

key_highlights: Fiery Cryptical > The Other One > Cryptical sandwich that drives the second set.
Post‑drums run (Nobody's Fault → Truckin' → Smokestack Lightning → Comes A Time) showcases tight band chemistry.
Brent’s “Rooster” keyboard solo adds spicy flavor and his vocal interlude creates memorable tension.
Strong encore with It’s All Over Now Baby Blue leaves the audience on a high note.
song_highlights: It's All Over Now Baby Blue

band_performance.Jerry: Consistently energetic and hot on guitar, delivering strong leads on Nobody's Fault But Mine and the post‑drums run; occasional vocal slip in Cryptical noted but overall a solid performance.
band_performance.Bob: Enthusiastically praised for his rhythm work, adding depth to the bluesy numbers and jam sections.
band_performance.Brent: Spicy Rooster solo and quirky keyboard textures stand out; his brief vocal moment sparked on‑stage tension but contributed character to the show.
band_performance.Billy: Drums drive the high‑energy moments, especially during the post‑drums sequence and the Cryptical suite; noted for a balanced presence that supports rather than overwhelms.

must_listen_sequences: It's All Over Now Baby Blue
